software_item.name: actions
software_item.description: Patient action management service that creates and executes clinical workflow actions including bookings, appointments, referrals, and triage escalations
software_item.safety_class_rationale: Software that can contribute to hazardous situations through incorrect action execution (wrong appointments, failed referrals, missed escalations) but is not the sole cause of harm. Clinical decisions originate from triage/interviewer services; actions service executes those decisions.

hazard_contributions.description: Action executed when already executed/cancelled/expired causing duplicate clinical interventions
hazard_contributions.cause: Race condition or state validation bypass allowing multiple executions of same action
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Duplicate appointments, multiple referrals, redundant escalations leading to patient confusion, wasted resources, or conflicting care plans

hazard_contributions.description: Appointment created without required patient or condition ID
hazard_contributions.cause: Validation logic in accept() method bypassed or misconfigured action template
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Appointment created for wrong patient or without clinical context, leading to inappropriate care delivery

hazard_contributions.description: Booking created with invalid or unavailable timeslot
hazard_contributions.cause: Timeslot availability not re-validated before booking creation, or timeslot already taken by another patient
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Double-booked appointments causing patient unable to access care at expected time, delayed treatment

hazard_contributions.description: Referral created without triage interview ID
hazard_contributions.cause: Missing validation or incorrect reference ID extraction from action
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Referral lacks clinical context, practitioner cannot understand patient needs, inappropriate care delivered

hazard_contributions.description: Action execution partially succeeds then fails, leaving system in inconsistent state
hazard_contributions.cause: Rollback mechanism fails to clean up successfully executed sub-actions (booking created but appointment creation fails)
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Patient has booking but no appointment, or vice versa, leading to administrative errors and missed care

hazard_contributions.description: Action executed with wrong care unit assignment
hazard_contributions.cause: Care unit ID resolution logic selects incorrect care unit from multiple sources (action, option, state parameters)
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Patient referred to wrong care provider, delayed or inappropriate care delivery

hazard_contributions.description: Action expires before patient can execute, blocking urgent care access
hazard_contributions.cause: TTL (time-to-live) misconfigured or validation logic prevents action modification when needed
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Patient unable to book urgent appointment, delayed care for acute conditions

hazard_contributions.description: Wrong patient executes action due to patient ID mismatch
hazard_contributions.cause: Patient verification bypassed or actor role incorrectly mapped to patient ID
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Patient accesses another patient's care plan, confidentiality breach, wrong treatment delivered

hazard_contributions.description: Action validation fails silently allowing misconfigured actions to be created
hazard_contributions.cause: Exception handling suppresses validation errors or logging insufficient to detect configuration issues
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Actions created with invalid configuration execute incorrectly, leading to various downstream failures

hazard_contributions.description: Upstream service failure (health-manager, bookings, interviewer) causes action execution to fail without proper error communication
hazard_contributions.cause: Exception mapping incomplete or error messages not propagated to user
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Patient believes action succeeded when it failed, or receives unclear error, leading to missed appointments or delayed care

hazard_contributions.description: Action autoSelect flag incorrectly set causing automatic execution without user confirmation
hazard_contributions.cause: Action template configuration error or validation logic failure
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Patient automatically assigned to appointment or care pathway without explicit consent, inappropriate care delivery

hazard_contributions.description: Database transaction failure leaves action in inconsistent state
hazard_contributions.cause: Version conflict during concurrent action updates or database connection loss
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Action state (executed, cancelled) not properly persisted, patient may re-execute or lose access to care

risk_controls_implemented.implementation: Action.assertActive() checks status against executedAt, cancelledAt, validFrom, expiresAt before allowing execution
ActionExecutionManager checks if action status is DONE and returns existing result if same option executed

risk_controls_implemented.description: Patient ID and condition ID validation before appointment creation
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: CreateAppointmentActionExecutorImpl.accept() throws exception if patientId or conditionId is null

risk_controls_implemented.description: Patient verification before action execution or cancellation
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: Action.verifyPatient() validates that the patient executing/cancelling matches the action's patientId

risk_controls_implemented.description: Rollback mechanism for failed action execution
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: Each executor implements rollback() to undo changes (cancel booking, cancel appointment) when execution chain fails

risk_controls_implemented.description: Care unit ID validation before booking creation
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: CreateBookingActionExecutorImpl.accept() throws exception if careUnitId is blank

risk_controls_implemented.description: Triage interview ID validation before referral creation
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: CreateReferralActionExecutorImpl.accept() throws exception if triageInterviewId is empty

risk_controls_implemented.description: Action configuration validation at creation time
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: ActionValidator validates action type, options, autoSelect flag, expiration time, and triage outcome configuration

risk_controls_implemented.description: Exception mapping for upstream service failures
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: Service-specific exception mappers (HealthManagerExecutionExceptionMapper, BookingsExecutionExceptionMapper) convert upstream errors to user-facing messages
Extensive logging at debug/warn levels for action execution, executor acceptance, failures, and rollbacks

risk_controls_implemented.description: Metrics and monitoring for action execution
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: Micrometer timers and counters track action execution, executor results, rollback failures
Action entity has @Version field to detect concurrent modifications and prevent lost updates

risk_controls_implemented.description: Error state persistence and retry indication
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: ExecutionError includes isRefreshAction flag to indicate if user should retry. Failed execution state persisted to failedExecutionAt field.

risk_controls_implemented.description: Action event notification for status changes
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: ActionNotifier publishes events when action status changes to DONE or FAILED, enabling downstream monitoring

risk_controls_required_upstream.description: User training on action expiration and time sensitivity
risk_controls_required_upstream.rationale: Software cannot force users to execute actions before expiration; requires operational awareness

risk_controls_required_upstream.description: Monitoring and alerting for high rollback rates
risk_controls_required_upstream.rationale: Individual rollback failures are logged but system-level patterns require centralized monitoring

risk_controls_required_upstream.description: Action template review and approval process
risk_controls_required_upstream.rationale: Software validates structure but cannot assess clinical appropriateness of action templates

risk_controls_required_upstream.description: SLA monitoring for upstream service dependencies
risk_controls_required_upstream.rationale: Actions service depends on health-manager, bookings, interviewer availability; requires infrastructure-level monitoring
Action state persistence relies on database availability; requires infrastructure-level controls

failure_modes.name: Service crashes during action execution
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: Unhandled exception in executor causes service crash, transaction rollback
failure_modes.effect_on_system: Action remains in ACTIVE state, patient may retry and potentially succeed, or action expires
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: Exception logging, service health checks, restart metrics
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: Service auto-restart via Kubernetes, patient retries action or requests new one

failure_modes.name: Database connection lost during action save
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: Action execution succeeds but state not persisted, appears still ACTIVE
failure_modes.effect_on_system: Downstream services may have created booking/appointment but actions service doesn't record it; patient sees stale state
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: Database connection error logs, persistence layer exceptions
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: Manual intervention to reconcile action state with downstream services, or patient re-executes

failure_modes.name: Health-manager service unavailable
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: ProcessingException or WebApplicationException caught, action marked as failed execution
failure_modes.effect_on_system: Patient cannot create appointment or referral, must retry later or seek alternative care pathway
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: Exception logging with service name, HTTP error codes, retry flags
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: Patient retries when service available; urgent cases may require manual escalation

failure_modes.name: Bookings service unavailable
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: WebApplicationException or ProcessingException caught, timeslot creation rolled back if created
failure_modes.effect_on_system: Patient cannot book appointment, must retry or use alternative access channel
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: Exception mapping to user-facing error, logging, metrics for bookings failures
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: Patient retries when bookings service available; SelectTimeSlot task may be reset to allow new timeslot selection

failure_modes.name: Timeslot already taken by another patient
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: Bookings service returns TIMESLOT_ALREADY_TAKEN error, SelectTimeSlot task reset to unfinished
failure_modes.effect_on_system: Patient prompted to select new timeslot; if action expires before selection, patient loses access
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: Specific error type from bookings service, task state modification
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: User selects different timeslot; action refreshed to allow retry

failure_modes.name: Action template misconfigured
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: ActionsException thrown during action creation, action not created
failure_modes.effect_on_system: User workflow interrupted, cannot proceed with intended action; may require admin intervention
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: ActionValidator throws exception with specific error type, logged at warn level
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: Configuration must be fixed by administrator; user may need to restart workflow

failure_modes.name: Rollback fails after execution failure
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: Exception during rollback is caught and logged, does not propagate; action marked as failed
failure_modes.effect_on_system: Orphaned booking or appointment remains in downstream service, requires manual cleanup
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: Rollback failure logged at warn level with specific resource ID, rollback_failed metric incremented
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: Manual reconciliation required; monitoring alerts for high rollback failure rates

failure_modes.name: Concurrent action execution attempt
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: Optimistic locking exception on save, transaction rolled back
failure_modes.effect_on_system: One execution succeeds, concurrent attempt fails; patient may see error or success depending on which request they initiated
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: OptimisticLockException from JPA, database version conflict
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: Failed execution rolled back; patient may retry if their request failed

failure_modes.name: Patient verification fails
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: ActionsException thrown with FORBIDDEN error type, execution aborted
failure_modes.effect_on_system: Patient blocked from executing/cancelling action; logged as potential security issue
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: Exception with patient ID mismatch logged at warn level
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: Access denied; user must authenticate as correct patient

failure_modes.name: Invalid origin or option configuration
failure_modes.effect_on_system: User cannot proceed with workflow, may be blocked from accessing care
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: Validation exception logged with origin ID and action details
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: Configuration must be fixed; user may need to contact support

failure_modes.name: Interview service failure when fetching triage result
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: Exception caught, referral created without patient recommendation text
failure_modes.effect_on_system: Referral lacks clinical context (selfCareText), may reduce quality of practitioner review
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: Null check on interviewResult, logged implicitly via null handling
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: Referral still created; practitioner accesses triage interview directly if needed

failure_modes.name: JMS message queue failure
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: Action event notification not sent, action state still persisted
failure_modes.effect_on_system: Downstream consumers may not receive status update, potential data inconsistency
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: JMS client exceptions, message queue monitoring
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: Retry mechanism at JMS level; eventual consistency via polling or manual reconciliation

interfaces_with_safety_impact.safety_impact: Appointment or referral creation failure prevents patient from accessing care; incorrect data sent could result in wrong care delivery
interfaces_with_safety_impact.failure_handling: WebApplicationException and ProcessingException caught, mapped to user-facing errors, rollback triggered
interfaces_with_safety_impact.retry_policy: No automatic retry; user must manually retry based on error message

interfaces_with_safety_impact.safety_impact: Booking creation failure prevents patient from securing appointment slot; timeslot conflicts could cause double-bookings
interfaces_with_safety_impact.failure_handling: WebApplicationException and ProcessingException caught, timeslot creation rolled back, error mapped and presented to user
interfaces_with_safety_impact.retry_policy: Specific handling for TIMESLOT_ALREADY_TAKEN resets SelectTimeSlot task; general failures require user retry

interfaces_with_safety_impact.safety_impact: Failure to retrieve interview results means referrals lack clinical context; could result in inappropriate care decisions
interfaces_with_safety_impact.failure_handling: Null check on response, referral created without patient recommendation if interview unavailable
interfaces_with_safety_impact.retry_policy: No retry; referral proceeds without recommendation text

interfaces_with_safety_impact.safety_impact: Failure to validate practitioner capabilities could result in appointments with practitioners lacking required skills
interfaces_with_safety_impact.failure_handling: Used in validation only; failure logs warning but does not block action creation (depending on feature flag)
interfaces_with_safety_impact.retry_policy: No retry; validation may be skipped on failure

interfaces_with_safety_impact.safety_impact: Loss of action state data could result in duplicate executions or patient unable to access created resources (appointments, bookings)
interfaces_with_safety_impact.failure_handling: JPA exceptions propagate to caller; optimistic locking prevents lost updates; transaction rollback on failures
interfaces_with_safety_impact.retry_policy: No application-level retry; connection pool handles transient failures

interfaces_with_safety_impact.safety_impact: Failed event notifications mean downstream systems may have stale action status data
interfaces_with_safety_impact.failure_handling: Assumed handled by JMS infrastructure; no explicit error handling visible in code
interfaces_with_safety_impact.retry_policy: JMS broker-level retry and dead-letter queue

interfaces_with_safety_impact.safety_impact: Misconfigured action templates could result in invalid actions being created; unavailable templates block action creation
interfaces_with_safety_impact.failure_handling: Falls back to system default templates if partner-specific templates unavailable
interfaces_with_safety_impact.retry_policy: No explicit retry; fallback to system defaults provides resilience

interfaces_with_safety_impact.safety_impact: Plugin failures could prevent actions requiring external system coordination (e.g., booking in external EMR)
interfaces_with_safety_impact.failure_handling: PluginActionExecutorImpl handles exceptions; specific error mapping depends on plugin implementation
interfaces_with_safety_impact.retry_policy: No automatic retry; user must retry action

verification_gaps.description: State validation logic (Action.assertActive) lacks comprehensive unit tests for edge cases (concurrent modification, timezone handling)
verification_gaps.recommendation: Add unit tests covering: concurrent execution attempts, expiration at exact boundary times, clock skew scenarios

verification_gaps.description: Rollback mechanism success rate not verified via integration tests
verification_gaps.recommendation: Add integration tests simulating execution failures at each executor in chain; verify all upstream changes are rolled back

verification_gaps.description: JMS event notification delivery not verified in production-like environment
verification_gaps.recommendation: Add integration tests verifying ActionStatusEvent published for DONE and FAILED states; test broker unavailability scenario

verification_gaps.description: Database transaction failure scenarios not tested (connection loss during save, optimistic lock conflicts)
verification_gaps.recommendation: Add chaos testing for database failures; verify action state consistency when persistence fails after downstream service calls succeed

verification_gaps.description: Exception mapping completeness not verified - some upstream service errors may not map to user-friendly messages
verification_gaps.recommendation: Catalog all possible upstream error responses; verify each maps to appropriate ExecutionError with user-facing message

verification_gaps.description: Care unit resolution logic from multiple sources (action, option, state params) not fully tested for conflict scenarios
verification_gaps.recommendation: Add tests covering: conflicting care unit IDs from different sources, null handling, priority resolution

verification_gaps.description: Plugin service timeout and retry behavior not verified
verification_gaps.recommendation: Add integration tests with mock plugin services simulating slow responses, timeouts, transient failures

verification_gaps.description: Action template validation does not have tests covering all validation rules (autoSelect, option count, skip-to-drop-in combinations)
verification_gaps.recommendation: Add parametrized tests covering all ActionValidator validation rules with valid and invalid inputs

shared_hazards: Upstream service unavailability (health-manager, bookings, interviewer, directory)
Database connection loss during critical operations
Action state inconsistency across distributed system
system_level_controls_needed: End-to-end monitoring of action lifecycle from creation through execution to downstream resource creation
Alerting for high rates of action execution failures or rollback failures
Reconciliation process for orphaned bookings/appointments when rollback fails
SLA monitoring and circuit breakers for upstream service dependencies
Action template governance process with clinical review before deployment
Regular audit of action expiration rates and patterns indicating usability issues
